_README: Canonical input registry. New tools SHOULD reuse these input `id`s and constraints verbatim. Two tools that share an id collapse into a single column in the /pro batch grid; if they also share type + constraints (number min/max/step, select options, color palette), that column becomes bulk-writable — one value fills every row. The catalog validator (scripts/validate-catalog.js) emits a WARNING (never an error) when a tool uses one of these ids with a divergent type or constraints, so drift is visible without blocking. Labels here are advisory: a tool may show its own label (the /pro header uses the first non-empty one). Adding a new shared input? Add it here first, then adopt it. This is deliberately data, not docs — the authoring guide will link here.

body.label: Body
body.description: Main body copy (markdown where the tool renders it).

imageFraming.label: Zoom & Position
imageFraming.description: Zoom + pan of an image within its frame (like object-position with a scale). Tools that only pan may omit the `zoom` field.

conventions.typography: Per-element typography numbers use `<element>FontSize` and `<element>FontWeight` (e.g. headingFontSize, bodyFontWeight). Weight is a number with min 100, max 900, step 100. Size is a relative number whose range the tool defines.
conventions.bulkWrite: A /pro column is bulk-writable only when every tool that declares the id agrees on type AND constraints — number min/max/step, select option values, color palette. Match the values above to keep the column actionable.
